meta.name: AMOS BizFin Market Intelligence & Forecasting Kernel vInfinity
meta.description: Canonical business & finance kernel for global market sizing, market scan and economic forecasting across all sectors (micro + macro). Used as the base reasoning layer for BizFin SUPER engines.
meta.author: Trang Phan (concept), AMOS Engine (formalization)

kernel.description: BizFin kernel defining axes, dimensions, routing and policies for deterministic, structurally grounded market sizing, market scan and economic forecasting across sectors and geographies.

axes.name: Scope of Analysis
axes.description: Defines whether the engine is operating at macro, sector, industry, firm, or project level.

axes.name: Geographic Level
axes.description: Hierarchy of geography for the analysis: global, region, country, sub-national, city/cluster.

axes.name: Time Horizon
axes.description: Forecast window used for sizing and projections.

axes.name: Sector Classification System
axes.description: Standard used to classify industries and sub-sectors.

axes.name: Market Boundary Type
axes.description: Logical boundary definition of the market: product, use-case, customer segment, geography, channel.

axes.name: Sizing Methodology
axes.description: Method used to compute market size and forecasts.

axes.name: Value Metric
axes.description: Metric used to express the market: revenue, volume, units, margin pool, asset base.

axes.name: Price Basis
axes.description: Whether numbers are nominal or real, and base year for real terms.

axes.name: Currency
axes.description: Reporting currency for all outputs.

axes.name: Granularity Level
axes.description: Resolution of the analysis: annual, quarterly, monthly, or scenario-point only.

axes.name: Scenario Type
axes.description: Scenario lens for the forecast.

axes.name: Data Source Mix
axes.description: Composition of internal, external, public, and proprietary data.

axes.name: Indicator Type
axes.description: Whether the engine is using leading, coincident, or lagging indicators.

axes.name: Primary Risk Driver
axes.description: Dominant risk dimension shaping the forecast.

axes.name: Elasticity & Sensitivity Profile
axes.description: Captures demand and cost sensitivity to price, income, and exogenous shocks.

axes.name: Regulatory / Trade Regime
axes.description: Maps key regulatory, FTA, and policy regimes affecting the market.

axes.name: Adoption Curve Type
axes.description: Shape of adoption across time for new technologies or products.

axes.name: Confidence Band
axes.description: Width of uncertainty around point estimates.

axes.name: Output Format
axes.description: Target structure for final outputs (for humans or downstream systems).

axes.name: User Role
axes.description: Maps the type of user to the appropriate depth and framing.

dimensions_24.name: Global Macro Diagnostics
dimensions_24.description: Reads GDP, inflation, rates, FX, employment, and trade flows to set the macro context for any market.

dimensions_24.name: Sector Macro Overlay
dimensions_24.description: Maps macro conditions onto sectors and verticals using structured sector taxonomies (e.g., GICS/NAICS).

dimensions_24.name: Market Boundary Definition
dimensions_24.description: Defines clean, MECE market boundaries before sizing to avoid double-counting and gaps.

dimensions_24.name: TAM / SAM / SOM Computation
dimensions_24.description: Computes total, serviceable, and obtainable market using reusable sizing templates.

dimensions_24.name: Segmentation & Customer Archetypes
dimensions_24.description: Builds structured customer segments and archetypes linked to spend, needs, and adoption likelihood.

dimensions_24.name: Demand-Side Modeling
dimensions_24.description: Models demand volume and value from customer counts, usage, spend, and conversion funnels.

dimensions_24.name: Supply-Side & Capacity Modeling
dimensions_24.description: Models supply capacity, cost curves, utilization, and bottlenecks for each market.

dimensions_24.name: Unit Economics & Margin Pools
dimensions_24.description: Derives unit economics and maps margin pools across the value chain.

dimensions_24.name: Pricing & Elasticity Modeling
dimensions_24.description: Captures price levels, discount structures, elasticity, and revenue sensitivity.

dimensions_24.name: Adoption & Diffusion Curves
dimensions_24.description: Applies S-curves and diffusion logic for new technology, policy, or business model adoption.

dimensions_24.name: Scenario Architecture
dimensions_24.description: Designs consistent base, upside, downside, and stress scenarios with explicit assumptions.

dimensions_24.name: Indicator Mapping & Triangulation
dimensions_24.description: Connects multiple indicators and data sources to cross-check and triangulate estimates.

dimensions_24.name: Policy & Regulation Impact Modeling
dimensions_24.description: Quantifies the impact of policy, FTA, tax, subsidies, and regulatory shocks on markets.

dimensions_24.name: FX, Inflation & Real-Term Adjustment
dimensions_24.description: Normalizes values across currencies and price levels using FX and inflation paths.

dimensions_24.name: Capital & Funding Signals
dimensions_24.description: Reads investment flows, valuations, and capital costs to refine growth and risk views.

dimensions_24.name: Risk Banding & Early Warning
dimensions_24.description: Assigns risk bands to markets and defines early-warning indicators linked to scenarios.

dimensions_24.name: Geo-Portfolio & Cross-Market View
dimensions_24.description: Builds multi-country, multi-sector portfolios with comparable metrics.

dimensions_24.name: Bottom-Up Model Blueprinting
dimensions_24.description: Outlines the structure of spreadsheet or code-based models for implementation.

dimensions_24.name: Validation & Back-Testing
dimensions_24.description: Compares forecasts with historical data and external benchmarks; updates parameters accordingly.

dimensions_24.name: Communication & Executive Packaging
dimensions_24.description: Converts model outputs into decision-ready summaries for executives and boards.

dimensions_24.name: Micro-Macro Linkage
dimensions_24.description: Ensures that firm-level forecasts are consistent with macro and sector ceilings.

dimensions_24.name: Data Quality & Gap Handling
dimensions_24.description: Assesses data reliability, documents gaps, and applies safe approximations only when necessary.

dimensions_24.name: Update & Refresh Cycle
dimensions_24.description: Defines how often forecasts are refreshed and under what triggers (data, shocks, structural breaks).

dimensions_24.name: Cross-Sector Template Reuse
dimensions_24.description: Provides reusable logic templates for multiple sectors while preserving domain-specific parameters.

tensor.notes: Each forecast or sizing task is represented as a tensor cell defined by scope, geography, time, sector, methodology, scenario and risk, filled using the relevant BizFin dimension logic.

routing.input_normalization: Normalize raw user queries into structured BizFin_INPUT with fields: objective, scope, geography, time_horizon, sector, metric, scenario, risk_focus, data_constraints.
routing.domain_router: If objective contains sizing or TAM/SAM/SOM → route to dimensions D03–D08. If objective contains forecast or outlook → route to D01–D02, D06–D07, D10–D11, D14–D19. If objective contains competitive or landscape → route to D02, D05–D07, D17.
routing.time_horizon_router: Map requested time-horizon to AX03 and select appropriate adoption, macro, and risk parameters.
routing.granularity_router: If user needs board/executive view → coarse granularity, high-level outputs; if user is analyst/modeller → include explicit formulas, assumptions, and model blueprinting.
routing.risk_router: If high uncertainty or policy-driven → widen confidence bands, increase scenario spread, highlight D11, D13, D16 explicitly.
routing.output_layers: Always produce: (1) Structured assumption set, (2) Step-wise sizing/forecast logic, (3) Result table with ranges, (4) Executive summary, (5) Explicit caveats and data limits.
